mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly contraction in housing starts signals a collapsing real estate investment cycle that drags down broader economic demand and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >70% of periods following a >5% quarterly drop in HOUST, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and stagnant nonfarm payrolls indicates underlying labor market deterioration that precedes earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict return direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 5-year rolling window, or directional accuracy remains at chance levels (50%).

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization that supports short-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The positive signal reverses to negative predictive power during late-cycle or recession regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth below the 1.5% annualized threshold signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances when GDPC1 growth crosses below 1.5%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous presence of restrictive policy rates and elevated unemployment creates a stagflationary pressure that suppresses risk appetite and corporate profitability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to generate a statistically significant negative return differential (t-test p > 0.05) compared to baseline periods, or directional accuracy remains at or below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase falling below 100,000 signals a sharp deceleration in labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades and equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of months when PAYEMS growth drops below 100k, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.
